sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The game is over (GAME_OVER state). I can see from the segmentation that:
- Node 12: color=p, pixels=84 (purple frame area)
- Node 13: color=W, pixels=100 (main block - all white)
- Node 14: color=c, pixels=64 (timer bar - now full width, was shrinking)
The timer bar went from pixels=1 to pixels=64, which means it reset or the game over state changed it. The main block is all white (Node 13: W, 100 pixels).
But the image shows purple-top/white-bottom for the main block. Let me reconcile: the image shows the reference as white-top/purple-bottom, and the main block as purple-top/white-bottom. But the segmentation says Node 13 is all white (100 pixels). This is confusing.
Wait, looking at the segmentation more carefully:
- Node 12: color=p, pixels=84, boundary=[[25, 26], [25, 37], [31, 37], [31, 26]] - this is the purple area (rows 25-31, cols 26-37)
- Node 13: color=W, pixels=100, boundary=[[34, 27], [34, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] - this is the white area (rows 34-43, cols 27-36)
So the main block has:
- Purple area: rows 25-31 (7 rows), cols 26-37 (12 cols) = 84 pixels ✓
- White area: rows 34-43 (10 rows), cols 27-36 (10 cols) = 100 pixels ✓
Wait, but the main block should be 10x10 = 100 pixels total. But here we have 84 + 100 = 184 pixels. That doesn't add up. Let me re-examine.
Actually, Node 12 has boundary [[25, 26], [25, 37], [31, 37], [31, 26]], which is rows 25-31 (7 rows) and cols 26-37 (12 cols). But 7*12 = 84, which matches. And Node 13 has boundary [[34, 27], [34, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]], which is rows 34-43 (10 rows) and cols 27-36 (10 cols). 10*10 = 100, which matches.
But these are different sizes! Node 12 is 7x12 and Node 13 is 10x10. They're not the same block. Let me look at the image again.
Looking at the image, I see:
- A purple rectangle at the top of the main area
- A white square below it
- There's a gap between them (row 32-33?)
Wait, the boundaries show:
- Node 12: rows 25-31 (top purple area)
- Node 13: rows 34-43 (bottom white area)
- Gap: rows 32-33
So the main block is split into two parts with a gap. The top part is purple (7x12) and the bottom part is white (10x10). This is different from what I expected.
Actually, I think the game over state might have changed the board. Let me just try to continue playing since the game says "game_over" but still has valid actions. Maybe I can restart or continue.
Actually, looking at the last_action_result, it says 'state': 'GAME_OVER' and 'game_over': True. The game is truly over. I need to figure out how to restart.
Let me try pressing SPACE or some other action to see if the game restarts.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game is over. Let me try to restart by pressing SPACE or clicking somewhere.
print("Trying to restart...")
action(['SPACE'])
print(f"Level: {current_frame.level}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")

# Check if the board reset
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nNodes: {len(seg['nodes'])}")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
